[CV CONFIRMED FIELDS]
Name: Sarah Chen
Emails: sarah.chen@candidate-mail.example
Phones: +1 (415) 555-0172

[CV EDUCATION]
- B.S. Electrical Engineering and Computer Science | M.S. Computer Science, Pacific Coast University  2018 | M.S. Computer Science, Pacific Coast University  2018 | PUBLICATIONS AND LEADERSHIP
  • B.S. Electrical Engineering and Computer Science, Bay City Institute  2016
  • ●

[CV SKILLS]
- Weights & Biases
- feature stores
- experiment tracking
- CI for ML
- observability-driven rollout.

[CV RAW TEXT]
Synthetic candidate profile for demo use only. Page 1 Sarah Chen Senior ML Engineer  San Francisco, CA  sarah.chen@candidate-mail.example  +1 (415) 555-0172 SUMMARY Senior ML engineer with 8 years of experience across research, model optimization, and production systems. Strong record in transformer-based workloads, large-scale inference, and technical leadership in fast-moving product environments. CORE STRENGTHS ● Transformer architectures, instruction tuning, evaluation design, and training-cost optimisation. ● Production ML systems with 10M+ daily predictions and modern MLOps tooling. ● Technical leadership for small ML teams, mentorship, and cross-functional delivery. ● Research communication, publication writing, and experiment-driven iteration. EXPERIENCE Aurora AI Systems — Senior ML Engineer  2022 — Present Led RLHF and evaluation pipeline improvements for enterprise language-assistance products; reduced training and evaluation costs by 34%; partnered with product and infra teams to roll out production model updates with audit-friendly change logs. Atlas Data Robotics — ML Engineer  2020 — 2022 Built data quality scoring and annotation automation for large-scale ML workflows; supported high-throughput inference systems serving more than 10M daily predictions; collaborated closely with product and operations stakeholders. Northstar Research Labs — Research Engineer  2018 — 2020 Contributed to transformer-model experiments, published two peer-reviewed papers on attention mechanisms, and mentored interns on evaluation pipelines and reproducibility practices. EDUCATION M.S. Computer Science, Pacific Coast University  2018 B.S. Electrical Engineering and Computer Science, Bay City Institute  2016 PUBLICATIONS AND LEADERSHIP ● Five peer-reviewed conference papers across model evaluation and efficient transformer training. ● Led a five-person ML pod during a product-scale rollout at Aurora AI Systems. ● Mentored three interns and two junior engineers on experimentation, tooling, and model release process. TOOLS AND SYSTEMS PyTorch, JAX, Kubeflow, MLflow, Weights & Biases, Kubernetes, feature stores, experiment tracking, CI for ML, observability-driven rollout.

indexing_payload.raw_text:
Synthetic candidate profile for demo use only.

Page 1

Sarah Chen

Senior ML Engineer  San Francisco, CA  sarah.chen@candidate-mail.example  +1 (415) 555-0172

SUMMARY

Senior ML engineer with 8 years of experience across research, model optimization, and production systems. Strong record in

transformer-based workloads, large-scale inference, and technical leadership in fast-moving product environments.

CORE STRENGTHS
●

Transformer architectures, instruction tuning, evaluation design, and training-cost optimisation.
●

Production ML systems with 10M+ daily predictions and modern MLOps tooling.
●

Technical leadership for small ML teams, mentorship, and cross-functional delivery.
●

Research communication, publication writing, and experiment-driven iteration.

EXPERIENCE

Aurora AI Systems — Senior ML Engineer  2022 — Present
Led RLHF and evaluation pipeline improvements for enterprise language-assistance products; reduced training and evaluation
costs by 34%; partnered with product and infra teams to roll out production model updates with audit-friendly change logs.

Atlas Data Robotics — ML Engineer  2020 — 2022
Built data quality scoring and annotation automation for large-scale ML workflows; supported high-throughput inference systems
serving more than 10M daily predictions; collaborated closely with product and operations stakeholders.

Northstar Research Labs — Research Engineer  2018 — 2020

Contributed to transformer-model experiments, published two peer-reviewed papers on attention mechanisms, and mentored
interns on evaluation pipelines and reproducibility practices.

EDUCATION

M.S. Computer Science, Pacific Coast University  2018

B.S. Electrical Engineering and Computer Science, Bay City Institute  2016

PUBLICATIONS AND LEADERSHIP
●

Five peer-reviewed conference papers across model evaluation and efficient transformer training.
●

Led a five-person ML pod during a product-scale rollout at Aurora AI Systems.
●

Mentored three interns and two junior engineers on experimentation, tooling, and model release process.

TOOLS AND SYSTEMS

PyTorch, JAX, Kubeflow, MLflow, Weights & Biases, Kubernetes, feature stores, experiment tracking, CI for ML,
observability-driven rollout.
